# bowtie_ipv6_range (Resource)

Manage an organization IPv6 address pool. Leave `range` unset to have the Controller generate a Bowtie ULA prefix. Destroying this resource deletes the range with Controller cascade behavior enabled so allocations from the pool do not block deletion.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Destroying this resource uses the Controller's cascade delete behavior so
# existing allocations from the pool do not block deletion.
resource "bowtie_ipv6_range" "lan" {
  # Leave range unset to let the Controller generate a Bowtie ULA prefix.
  assign_addresses_from_here = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `assign_addresses_from_here` (Boolean) Whether the Controller assigns addresses to devices from this pool.

### Optional

- `range` (String) The IPv6 CIDR for the pool, for example `fd00:1:1:5678::/64`. Generated by the Controller when unset.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `last_updated` (String) Provider metadata: the last time Terraform changed this object.
